Japanese: ２月１０日　午前　９時３９分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: February 10, 9:39 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: おはようございますっ！ ‥‥あれ。おひとり、なのですか？
English: Good morning! ...Oh? Are you by yourself?

Japanese: ああ‥‥おはよう、春美ちゃん。
English: Ah... Morning, Pearls.

Japanese: あ、あの！　真宵さまはっ！ ごブジでしたか！
English: M-Mr. Nick! Please tell me! What's going to happen to Mystic Maya?

Japanese: ‥‥それが‥‥ まだ、わからないんだ。
English: I'm sorry... We don't know yet.

Japanese: 『一般人は、立入禁止だ』って‥‥ 奥の院に入れてもらえなくて。
English: The investigation is still going on, so I wasn't allowed into the Inner Temple.

Japanese: そうなのですか‥‥
English: Oh, I see...

Japanese: それでは‥‥あやめさまは、 今も修験堂で《からくり錠》を？
English: So is Sister Iris still trying to remove those trick locks in the Training Hall?

Japanese: ううん‥‥彼女は、この事件の 被告人だからなあ。
English: No... She's the defendant in this case, so she can't be at the Inner Temple.

Japanese: さすがに、今は裁判所に 来ているはずだよ。
English: She's required to be here in court.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥なぜ、この 控え室にいないのでしょう。
English: Um... Then... How come she's not here in the defendant's lobby?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ こんなこと、初めてだな）
English: (I have to admit it's kinda strange...)

Japanese: ‥‥彼女ならば、 検察側の控え室にいる。
English: ...If you're looking for Iris, she's in the prosecutor's lobby.

Japanese: ‥‥み、御剣！
English: ...Ed-Edgeworth.

Japanese: あやめさんが‥‥なぜ 今ごろ、検察側に‥‥？
English: What's Iris doing over there?

Japanese: 今日の証言について、担当検事と 打ち合わせをしているところだ。
English: She's going over today's testimony with the prosecutor as we speak.

Japanese: “今日の証言”‥‥？
English: Today's testimony...?

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさんは‥‥
English: You heard me...

Japanese: 検察側の証人として、 証言台に立つようだ。
English: Iris is going to be testifying as a witness for the prosecution.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって！
English: Wait, what!?

Japanese: 何やら、検事が《告白》させる。 ‥‥そう聞いている。
English: The prosecutor is squeezing her for a confession. ...Or so I heard.

Japanese: （狩魔検事のやつ‥‥ どういうつもりだ‥‥？）
English: (Franziska von Karma... What are you up to...?)

Japanese: 成歩堂。‥‥今日、検事席に 立つのは、狩魔　冥ではない。
English: I know what you're thinking, but Franziska isn't going to be the prosecutor today.

Japanese: え‥‥！　じゃあ‥‥ いったい、だれが？
English: What...!? Then who is?

Japanese: 決まっている。 ‥‥ゴドー検事だ。
English: Who else would it be but Godot?

Japanese: ご、ゴドー‥‥‥
English: G-Godot...

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔　冥は今、修験洞で、 大切なシゴトをしている。
English: ...Franziska is engaged in some important work at the Sacred Cavern.

Japanese: しゅげんどう‥‥？ ま、まさか‥‥
English: The Sacred Cavern...? You don't mean that she's...

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Exactly.

Japanese: ゆうべからずっと《からくり錠》の 解除を行っているのだ。
English: She has been out there all night, trying to remove those trick locks.

Japanese: 住職の手ほどきを受けながら、な。
English: With the head nun's assistance, naturally.

Japanese: おそらく‥‥あと３時間もすれば、 すべての《錠》が開くだろう。
English: We estimate that the last of the locks should be taken care of in about three hours.

Japanese: ‥‥よい知らせが 届くことを祈っている。
English: I hope everything continues to go smoothly and we receive some good news soon.

Japanese: ああ‥‥‥ありがとう、御剣。
English: Yeah... Thanks, Edgeworth.

Japanese: ゴドー検事は、今日ですべての 決着をつけるつもりだ。
English: Prosecutor Godot intends to nail this case shut today.

Japanese: 《明日はない》‥‥ そう考えておくがいい。
English: Be prepared to fight like there's no tomorrow!

Japanese: ‥‥言われなくても わかってるさ。
English: ...You don't have to tell me that.

Japanese: フッ‥‥。その、目の色。
English: I know. I can already see it in your eyes.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥もう、熱で浮かされた きのうのキミではないようだ。
English: You're not the same fever-ridden, frantic maniac you were yesterday.

Japanese: ‥‥ここへ来たとき、決心した。 “今日こそ決着をつける”って。
English: It's strange... On the way here, I decided that today would be the end of all this.

Japanese: そうしたら‥‥急に、身体が ラクになったんだ。
English: Almost immediately after I made that decision, I felt myself getting stronger.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥
English: Interesting...

Japanese: 今ごろ、かわりにだれかが 熱を出しているかもしれないな。
English: Maybe you've passed your cold onto someone else, literally.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは‥‥
English: And with that...

Japanese: あとは、たのんだぞ。‥‥相棒。
English: I leave the rest in your capable hands... partner.

Japanese: ああ。‥‥まかせてくれ。
English: Thanks...

Japanese: （‥‥ほとんどのナゾには、 まだ答えが見つかっていない）
English: (...I still don't have answers for most of the riddles plaguing this case.)

Japanese: （エリス先生‥‥綾里　舞子が 殺害された状況‥‥）
English: (The circumstances around the murder of Ms. Elise Deauxnim, no, I mean Ms. Misty Fey...)

Japanese: （矢張が目撃した、 あり得ない光景‥‥）
English: (The impossible flight Larry claims to have seen...)

Japanese: （そして‥‥ あの女の、本当の“狙い”）
English: (And... What that woman is really after...)

Japanese: ‥‥そのすべてを‥‥ 今から、このぼくが解き明かす！
English: I will solve them all and bring this whole tragedy to an end!

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第７法廷
English: February 10, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 7

Japanese: これより、葉桜院　あやめの 法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Iris of Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: ‥‥あ、あの‥‥ どうして、裁判長が‥‥？
English: Umm... Your Honor...? Wh-What are you...?

Japanese: む。私ですかな？
English: Who? Me?

Japanese: じつは今朝がた、私の執務室に 弟の使いがやって来ましてね。
English: Well, my little brother came to visit me in my chambers earlier this morning.

Japanese: 急に、信じられない高熱で 倒れた、ということでした。
English: All of a sudden, in the blink of an eye, he developed a scorching fever and fainted!

Japanese: そこで、弟のかわりに、 この私が来たしだいです。
English: Therefore, I'll be standing in for him.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。 （兄弟だったのか‥‥）
English: I-I see, Your Honor... (So they're brothers! That explains a lot...)

Japanese: カオが、深ミドリ色でした。 かなりヤル気だったようです。
English: My poor brother. He looked a bit pale, not to mention sad that he couldn't be here.

Japanese: “先”のことは、わからねえ。 それが、人生ってヤツだ。
English: It is impossible to predict what the future has in store for any of us.

Japanese: だからこそ‥‥人は、自分の “過去”に決着をつけたがる。
English: This is precisely why people feel the need to judge the past.

Japanese: そいつが、オレたちに与えられた “特権”であり“使命”なのさ。
English: And we of the court have been charged with the solemn duty of passing such judgment.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。
English: ...Well said, Mr. Godot.

Japanese: 最後まで、わかったような わからないようなヒトコトですな。
English: I understood exactly what you said... at least up until the end, anyway.

Japanese: それでは、ゴドー検事。 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Now then, Mr. Godot. Please proceed with your opening statement.

Japanese: ‥‥人のココロは移ろいゆく。 それは、はかない夢のように‥‥
English: ...Humans are fragile, fickle beings. Our hearts change with the shifting of the tides.

Japanese: しがない世の中で、いつまでも 変わらないのは、ただひとつ。
English: There is only one thing that remains a constant in this crazy world...

Japanese: このカップに広がる 苦くて黒い闇だけ‥‥なのさ。
English: The bitter darkness that lies at the bottom of this mug.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり‥‥ どういうことですかな？
English: So then you mean...? Um... Forget it. What do you mean?

Japanese: 前回の審理で、被告人は 事件との関連を認めなかった。
English: During yesterday's trial, the accused refused to admit her role in the crime.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、今日。 彼女のココロは変わった。
English: But today, she has had a change of heart...

Japanese: 葉桜院　あやめには‥‥ 《告白》するべきことがあるのさ。
English: Sister Iris of Hazakura Temple has a confession to make.

Japanese: こ‥‥告白‥‥！ 被告人が‥‥ですか！
English: C-Confession...! Th-The defendant...?

Japanese: （あやめさん‥‥）
English: (Iris...)

Japanese: （‥‥なぜ、このぼくに 相談してくれなかったんだ‥‥）
English: (Why didn't she discuss this with me first...?)

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: それでは、被告人の《告白》を 聞かせていただきましょう！
English: This court will now hear the defendant's confession!

Japanese: ‥‥カワイコちゃんには、 名前と職業を聞く。
English: ...Upon meeting a beautiful lady, always ask for her name and profession.

Japanese: それが、オレのルールだぜ。
English: That's one of my rules.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。あやめ、と申します。
English: ...Um, my name is Iris.

Japanese: 葉桜院にて、修行中の 身でございます‥‥。
English: I am but a simple nun undergoing training at Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: 証人。あなたは‥‥何か 《告白》することがある、とか。
English: Witness, is there something that you want to confess to?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。成歩堂さまには 申しわけありませんが‥‥
English: ...Yes. But first, I want to apologize to Mr. Wright...

Japanese: もう、私‥‥かくしておくことは できません。
English: I... I can't continue lying to everyone anymore.

Japanese: い‥‥いったい、 なんのこと‥‥ですか？
English: I-It's alright... What is it...?

Japanese: 成歩堂さま。私‥‥この事件に、 無関係ではないのです。
English: Mr. Wright, I have to admit that I... I did play a part in this terrible incident.

Japanese: ‥‥まさか‥‥ 自白なさるつもりですかッ！
English: Are... Are you actually confessing!?

Japanese: あなたが、天流斎　エリスを 殺害した‥‥と！
English: Are you saying that you were the one who murdered Ms. Elise Deauxnim?

Japanese: ‥‥そうではありません。
English: ...No, I'm not, Your Honor.

Japanese: 私は‥‥そう。 あの事件の、後始末をしたのです。
English: But I dealt with the cover up, after the murder took place.

Japanese: あの方が命を奪った、天流斎 エリスさまの亡きがらを‥‥
English: After her spirit left, I took the lifeless shell of Mystic Elise...

Japanese: 葉桜院の境内に運んで、あのような 細工をさせていただきました。
English: ...and carried it to the Hazakura Temple Courtyard where I desecrated it.

Japanese: な‥‥‥ なんですっとォォッ！
English: Wh... Whaaaat!?

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-Order in the court! Order!

Japanese: つ、つまり‥‥被告人！ ‥‥あなたは‥‥
English: W-Witness...! Are you...

Japanese: この殺人事件の《共犯者》だった！ そうなのですかッ！
English: Are you saying you were an accomplice to the murder!?

Japanese: はい。そのとおりです。
English: Yes. That's correct.

Japanese: ‥‥えええええッ！
English: ...Whaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: （‥‥開廷３分で もう冷や汗まみれかよ‥‥）
English: (Three minutes in court and I'm already covered in a cold sweat...)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ 人はだれでも“共犯者”さ。
English: Ha...! Everyone on the planet is an accomplice to something.

Japanese: たまたま、それが殺人事件だった。 それだけのハナシ、だぜ。
English: It just happens to be that in this case, it's to murder.

Japanese: そうだろう？　‥‥まるほどう。
English: Isn't that right... Mr. Trite?

Japanese: （ゴドー検事め‥‥）
English: (Grrr, that Godot...)

Japanese: （打ち合わせをした“告白”‥‥ コイツのことかッ！）
English: (So this is the confession they were conferring about!)

Japanese: （どうやら、この あやめさんの証言書‥‥）
English: (It pains me to say this...)

Japanese: （‥‥悲しいけど、 “ウソ”だったんだな‥‥）
English: (...but it looks like Iris's testimony was all a lie.)

Japanese: 《あやめの証言書》を 丸めてポケットにつっこんだ。
English: Iris's Testimony crumpled up and shoved into a pocket.

Japanese: なあ、おじょうさんよォ。 よかったら‥‥教えてくれねえか。
English: Now then, little lady. If you don't mind, I've got a question for you.

Japanese: いったい、アンタ‥‥だれの犯行を モミ消そうとしたのか？
English: Whose crime were you trying to cover up by your actions?

Japanese: （あやめさんが、だれかを “かばっていた”‥‥）
English: (Iris was covering for someone...?)

Japanese: （ううう‥‥まったく先が 見えないぞ‥‥）
English: (Nngh... Now I'm definitely up the creek without a paddle... or a lifejacket.)

Japanese: 私‥‥おさないころから 葉桜院でお世話になっております。
English: ...I've been at Hazakura Temple ever since I was a little girl.

Japanese: 葉桜院は、倉院流霊媒道の分家。
English: Hazakura Temple is run by one of the branch families of the Kurain Tradition.

Japanese: 私どもは、本家を守るために 存在しているのです。
English: One of our missions is to protect the main family.

Japanese: ほ、“本家”‥‥ですか？
English: I'm sorry, but "main family"...?

Japanese: だから‥‥私。この身を汚しても、 “あの方”をお守りしたのです。
English: Yes, and that's why I would dirty myself, if need be, to protect her...

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道、家元の娘‥‥ 綾里　真宵さまを！
English: The daughter of the Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique, Mystic Maya Fey!
Huh...?

Japanese: そういうコト‥‥らしいぜ。 まるほどう‥‥。
English: Wake up and smell the coffee, Trite.

Japanese: ま‥‥‥‥ 真宵ちゃんですってェェェッ！
English: Sh-She's naming... MAYAAAAAAAAA!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にィッ！
English: Order! Order in the court!

Japanese: 殺害の現場を見ただけではなく‥‥ 真犯人の名前までッ！
English: So not only did you witness the murder... You know the name of the murderer!

Japanese: 本当に、ザンネンですけど‥‥ まちがいございません。
English: I'm terribly sorry... But it's true.

Japanese: 私は、あの方の犯行を 目撃して‥‥
English: I saw her commit the crime with my very own eyes...

Japanese: あの方のために、被害者の 亡きがらを始末したのです。
English: And then I cleaned up the area to try to protect her.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんなバカなッ！ 真宵ちゃんは、そんな悪
English: Th-That's ridiculous! Maya could never do such a--

Japanese: ‥‥今は、弁護人が声を張り上げる べき場面ではありません。
English: The defense will refrain from commenting until the appropriate time.

Japanese: 被告人。‥‥まずは、あなたの 証言を聞くべきでしょう。
English: Now, witness. Let's hear your testimony.

Japanese: 事件当夜‥‥いったい、 何があったのか‥‥？
English: What exactly happened on the night of the crime?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: ...Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: （あるていど、カクゴは していたつもりだったけど‥‥）
English: (I thought I was prepared for the unexpected...)

Japanese: （いきなり、予想もつかない 展開になってきたな‥‥）
English: (But I never imagined the case would wind up going in this direction...)

Japanese: ～真宵の《犯行》～
English: -- The Real Murderer --

Japanese: あの晩、奥の院へ行って‥‥ 中庭で、見てしまいました。
English: I went to the Inner Temple that night and I saw it all happen in the garden.

Japanese: エリスさまが、手に持ったツエで 真宵さまに殴りかかったのです！
English: I saw Mystic Elise strike Mystic Maya with her staff!

Japanese: 真宵さま、おヨロめきになって‥‥ エリスさまは、トドメを刺そうと！
English: While Mystic Maya was still stumbling, Mystic Elise moved in to deliver a fatal strike!

Japanese: 真宵さまは、それを必死にかわして 武器を奪って‥‥！
English: Mystic Maya tried desperately to defend herself and stole the weapon...!

Japanese: あれは、正当防衛でしたわ！ 真宵さまにセキニンはありません！
English: It was only in self-defense! You can't blame her for it!

Japanese: 《正当防衛》‥‥ですか。
English: So it was in self-defense?

Japanese: 襲いかかったのは、エリスさまの ほうだったのですから！
English: Yes. Mystic Elise was the one who attacked first!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: だから、私‥‥真宵さまを お助けしようと思ったのです。
English: That's why I tried my best to protect Mystic Maya!

Japanese: 被害者の死体を葉桜院に移せば、 綾里　真宵に疑いはかからない‥‥
English: You moved the victim's body to the temple so that Maya wouldn't be suspected...

Japanese: そう考えたわけだ。 悪くねえ悪知恵‥‥だぜ。
English: Isn't that right? Not bad... You've got the instincts of a true criminal.

Japanese: （‥‥おかしい。きのう、 たしか証明されたはずだ‥‥）
English: (Something's not quite right. I'm sure it was established yesterday...)

Japanese: （あの晩、あやめさんは 《奥の院へは行かなかった》）
English: (...that Iris never went to the Inner Temple that night.)

Japanese: （奥の院に現れたのは‥‥ “彼女”だったハズだ）
English: (And that the person who did go was... that woman...)

Japanese: （‥‥あやめさんも、それを 認めたはずなのに‥‥）
English: (...Iris even admitted it.)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人！ 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Now then, Mr. Wright. Please proceed with your cross-examination.

Japanese: ～真宵の《犯行》～
English: -- The Real Murderer --

Japanese: あの晩、奥の院へ行って‥‥ 中庭で、見てしまいました。
English: I went to the Inner Temple that night and I saw it all happen in the garden.

Japanese: エリスさまが、手に持ったツエで 真宵さまに殴りかかったのです！
English: I saw Mystic Elise strike Mystic Maya with her staff!

Japanese: 真宵さま、おヨロめきになって‥‥ エリスさまは、トドメを刺そうと！
English: While Mystic Maya was still stumbling, Mystic Elise moved in to deliver a fatal strike!

Japanese: 真宵さまは、それを必死にかわして 武器を奪って‥‥！
English: Mystic Maya tried desperately to defend herself and stole the weapon...!

Japanese: あれは、正当防衛でしたわ！ 真宵さまにセキニンはありません！
English: It was only in self-defense! You can't blame her for it!

Japanese: （この場で《霊媒》を持ち出すのは できれば、避けたい‥‥）
English: (I'd like to avoid the whole spirit channeling thing if I can...)

Japanese: （‥‥証拠として採用されるとは 考えられないし‥‥）
English: (Not only would it be inadmissible as evidence...)

Japanese: （裁判長の心証を 悪くする恐れがある）
English: (...but I think it might actually hurt our standing with the judge.)

Japanese: （‥‥まずは、この証言の 不自然な点を暴くとするか‥‥）
English: (First I need to pick her testimony apart and expose the unnatural-sounding parts...)

Japanese: （あやめさんがなぜ、こんな 証言をしたかはわからないけど）
English: (I don't know why Iris would tell a story like this...)

Japanese: （ここには、とても不自然な点が ２つ‥‥かくれている！）
English: (...but there are two things I noticed that just don't add up!)

Japanese: ジャマなツエは捨てて‥‥フトコロ から、小刀を取り出したのです！
English: She threw her staff away and pulled a dagger from inside her robe!

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさん。きのうと 証言が変わっているようですね。
English: Sister Iris! Your testimony has changed quite a bit since yesterday.

Japanese: 『奥の院には行かなかった』 ‥‥きのうは、そう言ってました！
English: You stated yesterday that, on the night of the murder, you didn't go to the Inner Temple.

Japanese: “きのう、何を言ったか‥‥？” そんなコト、なんのイミもねえぜ。
English: Did she now? Too bad for you, what she said yesterday doesn't mean much today.

Japanese: まるほどう‥‥。アンタ、 その話をどこで聞いたんだい？
English: By the way, where were you when she claimed that she didn't go?

Japanese: え。‥‥た、たしか‥‥ 奥の院の、修験堂でしたけど。
English: Umm, I... It was in the Inner Temple's Training Hall...

Japanese: 《証言》と呼べる発言は、 証言台でするもの、だぜ。
English: A private conversation between the two of you does not constitute testimony.

Japanese: それ以外は、すべて“おしゃべり” ‥‥なんの価値もねえさ。
English: That would be properly described as hearsay.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ いかがですか、証人。
English: Hmm... What do you have to say, witness?

Japanese: 私‥‥どうしても、 言えなかったのです。
English: I-I just couldn't tell him the truth at that time.

Japanese: 真宵さまは‥‥あなたの、 大切な方、なのですよね？
English: Mystic Maya... She's your girlfriend, isn't she?

Japanese: その方の犯行を目撃した、なんて。 私の口からは、とても‥‥
English: I... I didn't want to be the one to break it to you that I saw her commit murder...

Japanese: ‥‥わかるような気がします。
English: There, there... We all understand how difficult this is for you.

Japanese: それでは、証言を つづけていただきましょう。
English: Now then, let's continue with the testimony.

Japanese: あなたが、奥の院の中庭で 何を見たか‥‥？
English: What did you witness in the temple garden...?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。かしこまりました。
English: Well, Your Honor...

Japanese: 被害者が、真宵ちゃん‥‥ 綾里　真宵を襲ったんですか！
English: You're saying that the victim attacked Maya... I mean Ms. Fey?

Japanese: は‥‥はい。それは 恐ろしい光景でしたわ。
English: Y-Yes... It was a truly frightening scene.

Japanese: 真宵さまは、アタマを強く 殴られて、倒れそうになりました。
English: Mystic Maya was struck hard on the head and looked like she was going to collapse.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとき、あなたは何を？
English: What were you doing at the time?
Um...

Japanese: どうして、ふたりを 止めなかったんですか！
English: Why didn't you stop them from fighting!?

Japanese: も、申しわけありません。 成歩堂さま‥‥
English: I-I'm terribly sorry, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: 私‥‥恐ろしくて、足が すくんでしまって‥‥
English: I was... I was frightened... I couldn't move...

Japanese: 声を上げることすら できませんでしたの。
English: I couldn't even speak, I was in such shock...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ムリもないでしょうな。
English: Hmm... That's perfectly understandable, my dear.

Japanese: （ダメだ‥‥）
English: (This doesn't sound right...)

Japanese: （この証言だけは‥‥どうしても 信じることができない！）
English: (I don't believe this testimony for a minute!)

Japanese: ‥‥それから、 どうなったのですかな？
English: ...What happened after that?

Japanese: トドメを‥‥？
English: She moved in to deliver a fatal strike?

Japanese: ‥‥そうだと思います。
English: ...Yes, I'm sure of it.

Japanese: 手にしたツエを投げ捨てて、 ふところから、武器を‥‥
English: She threw down her staff and reached into her robe for a weapon...

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！ “武器”というのは‥‥？
English: Wait a minute! What was this weapon...?

Japanese: はい。あれは‥‥ 小刀のようなものでした。
English: It-It was... some kind of dagger.

Japanese: （小刀‥‥ねえ‥‥）
English: (A dagger, huh...?)

Japanese: その武器で、天流斎　エリスは 刺そうとしたんですか？
English: And Elise Deauxnim tried to stab her with this weapon?

Japanese: ‥‥その。綾里　真宵を。
English: ...To kill Ms. Fey?

Japanese: ええ‥‥そのとおりです。
English: Yes... Exactly.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
コーヒーとまちがえて、 ソースを飲んだみたいなカオだぜ。
English: You look like I did after I mistakenly took a swig of Worcestershire sauce.

Japanese: 自分の依頼人の発言が スッキリしねえか？　弁護士クン。
English: Do you have a problem with the testimony we're hearing from your client, Lawyer Boy?

Japanese: （今の、あやめさんの発言 ‥‥どうなんだろう？）
English: (Hmm... Do I have a problem with Iris's testimony...?)

Japanese: （‥‥あやめさんは、 ぼくの依頼人だ）
English: (Iris is my client...)

Japanese: （むやみにゆさぶらない ほうが、いいだろう‥‥）
English: (I'd better not press her too hard...)

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさんの発言に、 問題はないと思われます。
English: The defense has no objection to Ms. Iris's testimony.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
一生、アマいコーヒー牛乳を 飲むがいいぜ、まるほどう‥‥
English: You should try some nice, sweet café con leche at least once, Trite...

Japanese: それでは、証言を つづけていただきましょう。
English: Very well, let's continue with your testimony.

Japanese: 被害者の攻撃を受けた、 綾里　真宵さんは‥‥？
English: What happened to Ms. Fey when she was attacked by the victim?

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: ...Your Honor.

Japanese: ただ今の証人の発言には‥‥ “問題”があります！
English: I have a small problem with the witness's testimony.
Y-You do?

Japanese: しかし、この証人は あなたの依頼人ですぞ！
English: But this witness is your own client!

Japanese: ま、まあ。 そうなんですけど‥‥
English: Yes, well... N-Nevertheless...

Japanese: ‥‥いいだろうぜ。
English: ...That's fine.

Japanese: 証人。今の発言‥‥証言に 加えてくれねえか。
English: Witness, let's add your last statement to the testimony.

Japanese: は‥‥はい。 わかりました。
English: Y-Yes, sir.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと！　そのへんの 進行は、私がカツンとですね‥‥
English: H-Hey! Just a moment! It's my job to say that...

Japanese: ‥‥いいか、アンタ。 二度は言わねえ。
English: ...Listen, gramps. I won't say it again.

Japanese: 裁くのは、このオレだぜ！
English: Final judgment will be rendered by me!

Japanese: むぐぐ‥‥ッ！
English: Nnngghh...!

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、 つづけてもらおうか。
English: ...OK, now. Let's continue.

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それで‥‥？
English: ...And then?

Japanese: 真宵さまは‥‥エリスさまを 逆に追いつめました。そして‥‥
English: Mystic Maya, she turned things around and then...

Japanese: そのまま、手にした凶器で お刺しになったのです！
English: ...she used the weapon she had taken and lunged forward for a strike!

Japanese: ま‥‥まちがい ありませんか！　‥‥証人ッ！
English: W-Witness! Are you absolutely certain!?

Japanese: ええ‥‥。で、でも！ しかたがなかったと思います！
English: Yes... B-But believe me, she had no choice!

Japanese: だって‥‥もし、真宵さまが 何もなさらなかったら‥‥
English: If Mystic Maya hadn't done something...

Japanese: ‥‥この解剖記録の名前は、 《綾里　真宵》だった‥‥、か。
English: ...Maya Fey's name would've been written on this autopsy report instead.

Japanese: ふ‥‥ふむむむう‥‥
English: Hmm... Yes, I see...

Japanese: （マズいな‥‥）
English: (This is bad...)

Japanese: （事件の真相は、ぼくにも まだ、見当がつかないけど‥‥）
English: (I still can't put the pieces together...)

Japanese: （この証言にムジュンがなければ、 真宵ちゃんが犯人にされちまう）
English: (Unless I find a contradiction in her testimony, Maya's going to get blamed for this.)

Japanese: でも‥‥私。 これだけは、言えます。 I...
English: I only have this to say...

Japanese: ‥‥綾里　真宵が、 天流斎　エリスを刺した‥‥
English: ...So you're saying Maya Fey was the one who stabbed Ms. Elise Deauxnim.

Japanese: あやめさん！　本当に それを目撃したんですか？
English: Sister Iris! Is that really what you saw?
Yes...

Japanese: クドいぜ、まるほどう。
English: Tsk, tsk, tsk, Trite.

Japanese: カップの中には、ドス黒くニガい 疑念がウズまいていやがる。
English: If your mug is filled with black and bitter suspicions...

Japanese: その闇を払うには‥‥ 飲みほすしかねえのさ。
English: The only way to get rid of them is to drink them down quick...

Japanese: ‥‥こんなふうにッ！
English: ...Like this!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥あの。 どういうコト、ですか。
English: Umm... I know that's supposed be a metaphor, but I don't get it.

Japanese: アンタにできるのは、ただ１つ。 証拠を提示することだけ‥‥
English: There's only one thing you need to "get", Trite. It's called evidence...

Japanese: 早くしねえと‥‥ コイツがさめちまうだろうぜ！
English: Now hurry up... Before my next cup gets cold!

Japanese: （どういうことだ‥‥？）
English: (What does that mean...?)

Japanese: （ゴドー検事が‥‥ムジュンが 暴かれるのを待っている‥‥？）
English: (Is Godot waiting for me to point out the contradictions?)

Japanese: ツエを‥‥捨てた？
English: She threw her staff away?

Japanese: エリスさまは、真宵さまの 命を奪おうとなさったのです。
English: Mystic Elise was trying to kill Mystic Maya.

Japanese: だから‥‥凶器を 取り出したのだと思います。
English: I think that's why she wanted a more lethal weapon.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥。ツエでは、致命傷を 与えるのはムズカシイでしょう。
English: It's true... It's not easy to inflict a deadly blow with a staff.

Japanese: あやめさん。もう一度 カクニンします。
English: Sister Iris, let me make sure I've got this straight.

Japanese: 被害者が、ツエを 自分から捨てたんですか？
English: The victim herself chose to throw away her staff?

Japanese: は‥‥はい。 そうですけど‥‥
English: Y-Yes. That's what I saw...

Japanese: それが、どうかしましたかな？ ‥‥弁護人。
English: Is there anything odd about that, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥引っかかるな）
English: (Yeah, it's odd alright.)

Japanese: エリスさまは、小刀で 真宵さまに襲いかかったのです。
English: Mystic Elise attacked Mystic Maya with the dagger. I'm sure of this.

Japanese: あやめさん‥‥‥
English: Sister Iris...

Japanese: あなたの目撃した犯行現場には、 どうしてもムリがあります。
English: There's something strange about your version of events.
Huh...?

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスが、ツエを 捨てるはずがないんですよ。
English: Ms. Deauxnim throwing her staff away makes no sense at all to me.

Japanese: で‥‥でも！　ツエでは、 殴ることしかできませんから‥‥
English: B-But all you can do with a staff is hit someone...

Japanese: もちろん、あなたは知らない でしょうね‥‥あやめさん。
English: Naturally you wouldn't know this, Sister Iris, but...

Japanese: 被害者のツエには‥‥ ある細工がされていたんです。
English: ...the victim's staff had a special feature about it.

Japanese: ごらんのとおり。 カタナ‥‥です。
English: As you can see, it's a sword.

Japanese: あ‥‥っ！
天流斎　エリスが、綾里　真宵の 殺害を考えたのならば！
English: If Elise Deauxnim really had wanted to kill Maya Fey...

Japanese: 《小刀》など使う 必要は、なかった！
English: ...she wouldn't have needed to use a separate dagger!

Japanese: こんなにリッパなカタナを 持っていたのですからね！
English: Not when she already had a beautiful blade in her hands already!

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか？ あやめさん！
English: ...Well, Sister Iris? What do you have to say?

Japanese: あ、あの。私‥‥
English: Uhh, err, I...

Japanese: ‥‥アンタにしちゃア よくやったぜ、まるほどう‥‥
English: ...That was an impressive bit of investigating, Trite.

Japanese: このツエに、カタナが仕込まれて いたとはなあ。
English: I never would have thought there was a sword hidden in that staff.

Japanese: しかし‥‥それが なんだって言うんだ？
English: But even so... How should I put this?

Japanese: 互いのキョリが近い戦いならば、 長いカタナは扱いづらい。
English: A long sword is unwieldy, and thus quite ineffective in close-quarters combat.

Japanese: だから天流斎　エリスは、あえて 小刀を使ったのかもしれねえ。
English: Maybe that's why she chose a dagger over her blade.

Japanese: そ‥‥それは‥‥
English: Um, well...

Japanese: ‥‥重要なのは、凶器に 何を使ったか、じゃァねえのさ。
English: Anyway, the type of weapon she chose to use isn't what's important.

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスが、綾里　真宵を 殺害しようとした、という点だ。
English: The important thing is that she tried to kill Maya Fey.

Japanese: そこに不自然な点がないかぎり‥‥ 目撃証言に、モンダイはねえぜ。
English: As long as there's nothing strange about that, there's no problem with her testimony.

Japanese: （‥‥ちがう！　この証言は、 どう考えても不自然なんだ！）
English: (But there is something strange about this whole testimony!)

Japanese: いかがですかな？　弁護人。 検事の主張には、一理あります。
English: Well, Mr. Wright? The prosecution has a point.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。弁護側は、 証拠を提示したいと思います。
English: ...Very well, Your Honor. The defense will now present evidence to back its argument.

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂さま‥‥
English: M-Mr. Wright...!

Japanese: ‥‥証人の証言が“あり得ない” という証拠を‥‥‥もうひとつ。
English: I have here another piece of evidence that shows that this testimony can't be trusted.

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスが、綾里　真宵の 命を奪おうとするはずがない！
English: Because Ms. Elise Deauxnim would never attempt to take the life of Maya Fey!

Japanese: ‥‥‥ふむう‥‥‥ いかがですかな？　ゴドー検事。
English: Hmm... Well, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥
English: If you ask me...

Japanese: 不自然なのは、弁護士の 人差し指の角度、だったようだぜ。
English: ...the only thing that seems strange is the angle of Mr. Trite's index finger.
Eh...

Japanese: いっそ、その指‥‥ 天に向けたらどうだい。
English: Trite, sticking your finger in an electrical outlet might be safer for you.

Japanese: それなら‥‥ ダレもキズつかねえからな！
English: Otherwise it might get bitten off the next time you poke it in the wrong place!

Japanese: ぐううう‥‥‥ッ！
English: Arrrrgh...!

Japanese: （エリス先生が、真宵ちゃんを 襲うはずがない‥‥）
English: (I still don't think that Ms. Deauxnim would ever attack Maya...)

Japanese: （ふたりの関係を考えれば、 アキラカじゃないか‥‥！）
English: (If you think about their relationship, it's obvious!)

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、考えなおして いただきましょうかな。
English: ...Why don't you think it over one more time.

Japanese: ‥‥天流斎　エリスには、 綾里　真宵を襲えなかったのです！
English: ...Elise Deauxnim would never have attacked Maya Fey.

Japanese: ど‥‥どうして、そんなことが‥‥
English: Ho-How can you be so sure...?

Japanese: 《天流斎　エリス》という名前は、 本名ではありません。
English: Because the victim's real name was not "Elise Deauxnim".

Japanese: 彼女の本当の名前は‥‥ 《綾里　舞子》といいます。
English: Her real name was... "Misty Fey".

Japanese: あああっ！　ま、まさか‥‥！ “あやさと　まいこ”‥‥
English: Aaah! N-No...! N-Not Mystic Misty Fey!

Japanese: ど、どなたなのですかな？ 名字が、同じですが‥‥
English: Wh-Who is this Misty Fey? Is she related to...?

Japanese: 綾里　舞子は、倉院流霊媒道の 家元、霊媒師です。そして‥‥
English: Misty Fey is the Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique. She is also...

Japanese: 綾里　真宵の、実の母親なのです！
English: ...the mother of Maya Fey!

Japanese: な‥‥‥なんですって！
English: A-Are you serious!?

Japanese: あ‥‥あの、成歩堂さま！ それは‥‥本当なのですか？
English: Is it... really true? Mr. Wright...!?

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスが‥‥ 綾里　舞子さまだった、なんて！
English: Was Elise Deauxnim actually the great Mystic Misty!?

Japanese: まちがいありません。
English: There's no doubt about it.

Japanese: （あやめさん‥‥どうやら、 知らなかったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like Iris had no idea...)

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうだったのですか‥‥
English: I can hardly believe it...

Japanese: 母親が、１７年ぶりに会った ひとり娘を殺害しようとした‥‥
English: The idea that she would try to kill her only daughter, one she hadn't seen in 17 years...

Japanese: 検察側に、その理由が セツメイできるのですかッ！
English: Perhaps the prosecution can offer some explanation for why she would do such a thing!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛にィィ！
English: Order in the court!!

Japanese: たしかに、これは 《不自然》と言わねばなりません！
English: There's no doubt about it. This certainly is odd.

Japanese: 母親が、その娘の命を狙うなど‥‥ あってはならないことです！
English: I find it difficult to believe that a mother would try to kill her own daughter!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
“あってはならない”コトが、 本当に起こらなければ‥‥
English: If it turns out that I'm wrong about this...

Japanese: オレも明日から、カフェーの マスターになるぜ。
English: I'll quit my day job and open up a coffee house.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 親子に、悲しい事件は起こらない ‥‥オレもそう思いたいが‥‥
English: I'd like to believe that domestic violence cases like these never occur, but...

Japanese: 現実は、そう言い切れねえ。 だから、人はコーヒーを飲むのさ。
English: ...unfortunately, they're all too common. That's why people drink bitter coffee, you know.

Japanese: 意外な事実が判明しました。 しかし‥‥
English: You've brought to light a very interesting fact, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: 検察側の主張どおり、これだけでは ムジュンとは認められません。
English: But I must agree with the prosecution that domestic violence is not all that rare.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥！
English: Y-You don't mean...!?

Japanese: 被害者と綾里　真宵が親子である ことは、証言とは関係ありません。
English: The fact that they were mother and daughter doesn't itself disprove the witness's claims.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、証言に戻って いただいたほうが、いいようです。
English: I think it would be best if we returned to the testimony at hand.

Japanese: わ、わかりましたわ‥‥
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥もう１つ）
English: (Somehow...)

Japanese: （ベツのムジュンを見つける 必要があるみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like I'm gonna have to find another contradiction in her testimony.)

Japanese: （さらに《決定的》な ムジュンを‥‥！）
English: (Something more conclusive than this!)

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order in the court!

Japanese: 一見、証人の証言の流れは、 きわめて自然だと思われました。
English: Upon first hearing the witness's testimony, it seemed natural enough.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。
English: ...However.

Japanese: 《真実》を知ってしまえば、 不自然きわまりない‥‥
English: In light of some facts that have just been presented...

Japanese: 《カタナの仕込まれた武器である ツエを、わざわざ捨てた》
English: One, that the victim supposedly threw away a sword during a fight...

Japanese: 《現場で死闘をくり広げた２人が、 実の親子だった》‥‥
English: ...and two, that the two people battling to the death were mother and daughter...

Japanese: いずれも、“あり得ない”と までは言えませんが‥‥
English: Despite the facts being believable when taken on their own...

Japanese: 考えにくいことです！
English: ...when taken together, the entire story seems difficult to believe.

Japanese: ‥‥この世に、“あり得ない” ことなんて、何もねえさ。
English: ...Listen. There's nothing in this world that is impossible.

Japanese: ただ１つをのぞいて、な。
English: Except for one little thing.

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか？　ただ１つの “あり得ないこと”とは。
English: Yes? What is this one little impossible thing...?

Japanese: クッ‥‥。 まだ、わからねえのかい。
English: Ha...! You still don't get it.

Japanese: 《裏づけ調査をしないゴドー検事》 ‥‥だぜ。
English: You think maybe my beans are under-roasted, but you have no idea gramps.

Japanese: ‥‥‥あの。 どういうこと‥‥ですか？
English: Umm... Could you get to your point?

Japanese: オレが、この証人の“告白”を 聞いたのは、今朝のこと‥‥
English: I heard this witness's confession this morning...

Japanese: ８杯目のモーニングコーヒーを 飲みほしたばかりだった‥‥
English: ...just as I had taken the first sip of my eighth cup of morning coffee.

Japanese: ‥‥あなた、胃に穴があきますよ。
English: ...You're going to ruin your health, my friend.

Japanese: とにかく、この証人の 《告白》を聞いて‥‥
English: Anyway, after hearing this woman's confession...

Japanese: さっそく、捜査好きの刑事が 現場へ、すっ飛んでいった。
English: I had a detective who loves to investigate sent to the scene of the crime.

Japanese: そして‥‥刑事は発見した。 コイツを、な。
English: And... he discovered this little beauty.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ 証言にあった《小刀》ですかな？
English: Is that the dagger the witness testified to seeing...?

Japanese: 聞くまでもねえだろう。 それより‥‥気にならねえか？
English: Obviously, Your Honor... But do you not notice something else?

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに‥‥よく見ると、 “血痕”が付着しています！
English: ...Now that you mention it, if you look closely, there appears to be a bloodstain!

Japanese: い‥‥いったい、 どこにあったんですか？
English: Wh-Where did you find that?

Japanese: ぼくが現場を調べたときは、 そんなものはなかった！
English: I didn't see that when I investigated the crime scene!

Japanese: ‥‥アンタ、現場の 松の木は調べたかい‥‥？
English: ...Did you investigate the pine tree at the crime scene?

Japanese: 松の木‥‥？
English: Huh? The pine tree...?

Japanese: この小刀は‥‥松の木の ウラに、突き刺さっていた。
English: This dagger was stuck in the back side of the pine tree.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵と天流斎　エリスの 格闘に決着がついたとき‥‥
English: When the last blow was struck, ending the violent battle between the two women...

Japanese: コイツは、はね飛ばされたのさ。 ‥‥松の木に向かって、な。
English: ...this little baby was thrown in the direction of the back of the pine tree.

Japanese: ということは‥‥この血痕は、 被害者のものなのですね！
English: Which means... the blood on this dagger belongs to the victim, correct!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ アンタ‥‥聞いてなかったのかい？
English: Ha...! Were you even listening, old man?

Japanese: オレが“告白”を聞いたのは、 今朝のことだったんだぜ。
English: I first heard this confession this morning.

Japanese: １３杯目のモーニングコーヒーを 飲みほしたばかりだった。
English: Just as I had taken the first sip of my 13th cup of morning coffee.

Japanese: （‥‥さっきと 変わってるじゃないか！）
English: (Didn't you say it was your 8th just a few minutes ago!?)

Japanese: さすがに、血液検査をする 時間はなかったぜ。
English: I didn't have enough time to get the blood analyzed on such short notice.

Japanese: とにかく、その小刀を 提出してください！
English: In any case, the court will accept the dagger as evidence.

Japanese: この審理のあいだに、 本法廷が血液検査を手配します！
English: Furthermore, I order that a blood test be performed on it immediately!

Japanese: オレのコイビトだ。 ‥‥ダイジにあつかってくれよ。
English: This is my sweetheart. Make sure you treat her right.

Japanese: ‥‥係官！　大至急、この証拠品を 検査にまわすようにッ！
English: ...Bailiff! Get this piece of evidence to the crime lab for testing immediately!

Japanese: 証拠品《小刀》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Dagger added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Now then...

Japanese: ただ今の、証人の証言は やや不自然でした。
English: The testimony we've just heard had numerous unbelievable aspects to it.

Japanese: しかし！　こうして 現場から小刀が発見された以上‥‥
English: However! After having found the very dagger the witness spoke of...

Japanese: その証言は《真実》だと 考えるべきでしょう。
English: ...I believe we can consider her testimony to be credible.

Japanese: カワイコちゃんは、ウソをつかねえ ‥‥そいつが、オレのルールだぜ。
English: Cute girls never lie. Ever.

Japanese: それでは‥‥証人。さらなる 証言をおねがいしましょう。
English: In any case, witness... If you could please testify again to this court.

Japanese: あの‥‥何を、でしょうか？
English: Umm... About what, Your Honor?

Japanese: あなたが目撃した“事件”‥‥ ふたりの格闘について！
English: About the incident you saw... The battle between the two women!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: ...Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～ふたりの《格闘》～
English: -- The Battle --

Japanese: ツエで殴られた真宵さまは、一瞬 おヨロめきになったのですが‥‥
English: Mystic Maya stumbled briefly after being hit over the head with the staff...

Japanese: エリスさまの攻撃をかわして、 凶器を奪いとったのです！
English: But then she dodged Mystic Elise's next attack and stole her weapon!

Japanese: エリスさまは、逆に追いつめられ、 灯ろうを背にして立たされました。
English: Suddenly Mystic Elise was the one on the defensive, with her back to the stone lantern.

Japanese: 真宵さまは、そのまま小刀で エリスさまを刺したのです！
English: That's when Mystic Maya stabbed Mystic Elise!

Japanese: エリスさまは、小刀をふり払ったの ですが‥‥やがて、お倒れに‥‥。
English: Mystic Elise managed to fling the knife away, but then... Then... she collapsed.

Japanese: ‥‥いたましい事件です。
English: ...That was a very heart- breaking story.

Japanese: 親子のあいだに何があったのかは 存じあげませんが‥‥
English: I don't know if there were any bad feelings between them, but...

Japanese: 舞子さまが姿を消されてから、 １７年の月日が流れています。
English: It had been 17 years since Mystic Misty's disappearance.

Japanese: ‥‥おそらく、お互いに カオもわからなかったのでは‥‥
English: ...Perhaps they simply didn't recognize each other anymore...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥あり得ますな。
English: Hmm... That seems reasonable.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします！
English: Now then, Mr. Wright. Proceed with your cross-examination.

Japanese: ～ふたりの《格闘》～
English: -- The Battle --

Japanese: ツエで殴られた真宵さまは、一瞬 おヨロめきになったのですが‥‥
English: Mystic Maya stumbled briefly after being hit over the head with the staff...

Japanese: エリスさまの攻撃をかわして、 凶器を奪いとったのです！
English: But then she dodged Mystic Elise's next attack and stole her weapon!

Japanese: エリスさまは、逆に追いつめられ、 灯ろうを背にして立たされました。
English: Suddenly Mystic Elise was the one on the defensive, with her back to the stone lantern.

Japanese: 真宵さまは、そのまま小刀で エリスさまを刺したのです！
English: That's when Mystic Maya stabbed Mystic Elise!

Japanese: エリスさまは、小刀をふり払ったの ですが‥‥やがて、お倒れに‥‥。
English: Mystic Elise managed to fling the knife away, but then... Then... she collapsed.

Japanese: （‥‥一見、どこにも ムリのない証言に思える）
English: (It seems like a reasonable story at first...)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥１つだけ。ゼッタイに あり得ないことがある）
English: (But... there's one thing that is completely impossible.)

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんが、 だれかを刺すなんて‥‥）
English: (The very idea of Maya stabbing someone...)

Japanese: （それだけは、あり得ない！ だからこそ‥‥）
English: (That alone is impossible! Which means...)

Japanese: （この証言には、かならず ムジュンがあるはずだ‥‥）
English: (...this faulty testimony must contain another contradiction in it somewhere.)

Japanese: あなたは、それを どこから目撃したんですか？
English: Iris, where were you standing when you witnessed all this?

Japanese: あの‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Umm... What do you mean?

Japanese: もし、被害者たちが あなたに気づいていれば‥‥
English: Well, if either of them had noticed you...

Japanese: そんなハデな争いは 起こらなかったかもしれません。
English: ...they might not have continued their battle.

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだったの でしょうか‥‥。 I...
English: I wonder if that's true...

Japanese: おふたりは‥‥中庭の、灯ろうの あたりにいらっしゃいました。
English: The two of them... They were standing near the garden's stone lantern.

Japanese: 私‥‥それを、後ろから 目撃したのです。
English: I... I was watching the whole thing from behind.

Japanese: おふたりがいたところは 暗かったから‥‥
English: It was dark where the two of them were, so...

Japanese: それで、私のことが 見えなかったのだと思います。
English: I think that's why they didn't see me.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥事件当時、 中庭は、暗かった‥‥
English: Hmm... So it was dark in the garden when the murder took place...

Japanese: それで‥‥殴られた真宵さんは どうしたのですかな？
English: Tell me, what did Ms. Maya Fey do after she was struck?

Japanese: そのとき、被害者の ツエはどうなったのですか？
English: What happened to the victim's staff then?

Japanese: わ、わかりません。
English: I-I'm not sure.

Japanese: あたりは、とても暗かったので ‥‥私には、見えませんでした。
English: The area was very dark, so... I really couldn't see.

Japanese: お争いになるうちに、どこかへ 飛んでしまったのだと思います。
English: I think it got knocked away somewhere as the fight escalated.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど‥‥
English: That makes sense...

Japanese: それならば、小刀を取り出した セツメイもつきますな。
English: That would explain the need for the dagger.

Japanese: エリスさまは、真宵さまに 凶器の小刀を振り上げました。
English: Mystic Elise raised the dagger to strike.

Japanese: 真宵さまは、とっさに それをかわしたのです。
English: But Mystic Maya dodged it at the last second.

Japanese: 刃先が灯ろうに当たって、 小刀は地面に落ちました。
English: The dagger struck the lantern and fell to the ground.

Japanese: そして‥‥いつのまにか、 真宵さまが、その小刀を‥‥！
English: And then at some point, Mystic Maya picked it up...!

Japanese: （今のところ、不自然な点は 見あたらないな‥‥）
English: (Nothing sounds especially strange yet...)

Japanese: 凶器を奪われた被害者は、 どうなったのですかな？
English: What did the victim do when the weapon was stolen from her?

Japanese: 綾里　真宵が‥‥被害者を “追いつめた”‥‥？
English: You're saying that Maya Fey turned the tables on the victim?
Y-Yes...

Japanese: ‥‥真宵さまは、きっと 我を忘れていたのだと思います。
English: ...But Mystic Maya wasn't herself at the time.

Japanese: だって‥‥お命を 狙われたのですから‥‥
English: After all... Her life was in danger...

Japanese: （‥‥考えられない‥‥）
English: (I still can't believe it...)

Japanese: （彼女が、刃物を持って だれかを追いつめるなんて！）
English: (The idea of Maya cornering someone at knifepoint! It's just silly!)

Japanese: “追いつめられたネズミは、 ネコを噛む”‥‥そう言うぜ。
English: As they say... "A cornered fox is more dangerous than a jackal".

Japanese: でも、そのネズミは ネコを追いつめたりはしません！
English: I believe the correct description of a cornered fox is "scared and petrified".

Japanese: たとえ話なんか、 なんのイミもねえぜ。
English: Your animal analogies have grown tiresome!

Japanese: （そっちが始めたんじゃないか！）
English: (You were the one who started it!)

Japanese: それで‥‥
English: Anyway...

Japanese: 被害者を追いつめた、綾里　真宵 さんは‥‥どうしたのですかな？
English: So, after Maya Fey backed the victim into the stone lantern, what happened next?

Japanese: あなたは‥‥本当に、それを ハッキリ見たのですか！
English: Are you absolutely certain that's what you saw!?

Japanese: たぶん‥‥そうだと思います。
English: Yes, I'm almost certain.
"Almost"...?

Japanese: あのとき、中庭は 暗かったものですから‥‥
English: The garden was dark...

Japanese: それが“だれ”なのかは、 ほとんど見えなかったのです。
English: I couldn't really tell who was who.

Japanese: （なんだって‥‥？）
English: (What did she just say!?)

Japanese: そ‥‥それならば！ あるいは、別人だったかも‥‥
English: S-So in other words, it could have been someone else entirely...!

Japanese: ‥‥あの晩、綾里　真宵が 奥の院にいたのは、まちがいない。
English: We know that Maya Fey was at the Inner Temple that night.

Japanese: ‥‥そして、死んだのは 天流斎　エリスだ。
English: And that the woman that was killed was Elise Deauxnim.

Japanese: ‥‥だれとだれが争ったか‥‥ “見る”までもねえんじゃねえか？
English: Isn't it fairly obvious who attacked who?

Japanese: うううう‥‥ （反論できない。今は、まだ‥‥）
English: Nnrgh... (I don't have anything to counter that yet...)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証人。 証言をつづけてください。
English: ...Well then, witness. Please continue with your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。わかりました。
English: ...Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: おふたりは、しばらく 向かい合ったあと‥‥
English: The two of them faced each other for a moment...

Japanese: 《犯人》が、《被害者》に 体当たりしたのです！
English: Then Mystic Maya rushed straight into Mystic Elise!

Japanese: 《被害者》は‥‥身体を “く”の字に折って、倒れました。
English: Then Mystic Elise doubled over and fell to the ground.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 腹部を刺されたわけですな。
English: Hmm... So she was stabbed in the stomach, huh?

Japanese: ええ‥‥。
English: Yes...

Japanese: その、小刀は‥‥どこへ？
English: Where did that dagger go?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりません。 暗くて、見えませんでした。
English: ...I have no idea. It was dark and I couldn't see.

Japanese: コイツを見つけた刑事の 話によると‥‥
English: According to the detective who found the dagger...

Japanese: ‥‥小刀は、中庭の 松の木に刺さっていた。
English: ...it was stuck into the pine tree in the garden.

Japanese: ‥‥おそらく、そこへ 飛ばされたんだろうぜ。
English: Most likely it was flung towards the tree after the struggle.

Japanese: そう考えるのが 自然でしょうな。
English: That is the most likely explanation, yes.

Japanese: 腹部を刺された被害者は‥‥ すぐに倒れたんですか？
English: After being stabbed in the stomach, did the victim collapse right away?

Japanese: そういえば‥‥
English: Now that I think of it...

Japanese: しばらく、真宵さまのほうを 見つめていたような気がします。
English: ...she seemed to stare at Mystic Maya for a while.

Japanese: 真宵さまも、まったく 動かなくて‥‥
English: And Mystic Maya stood there, not moving an inch...

Japanese: そのまま、時が止まったような 気がしました。
English: It felt for a moment like time had just stopped.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: やがて‥‥魔法が解けたように エリスさまは、お倒れに‥‥。
English: But then, suddenly, as if the spell had worn off, Mystic Elise fell over...

Japanese: ‥‥今日のあなたは、どうも ようすがおかしいですね。
English: ...Something about you just isn't right today, Iris!
Huh...?

Japanese: きのうまでのあなたは、軽はずみな ことを言うひとではなかった。
English: Until now, I didn't think you were the type to make such a careless mistake.

Japanese: しかし！　‥‥今日の証言には、 あまりにムジュンが多い。
English: However! The testimony you just gave contains quite a few contradictions.

Japanese: そんな！　いったい‥‥ どこがおかしいのですかっ！
English: Wh-What do you mean? What's so wrong about my testimony?

Japanese: あなたの証言によれば、 綾里　真宵は‥‥
English: According to you...

Japanese: 『灯ろうを背にした被害者を、 小刀で刺した』‥‥そうですね？
English: ...Maya Fey stabbed the victim, who had her back to the stone lantern... Correct?

Japanese: そ、そうですけど‥‥
English: Y-Yes... That's right.

Japanese: その場合‥‥被害者は《腹部》を 刺されることになります。
English: But in that case, the victim would've been stabbed in the stomach, right?

Japanese: ‥‥それは、そうでしょうな。
English: Y-Yes, I think so...

Japanese: しかし！　解剖記録には、 こう書かれています。
English: But! According to the autopsy report...

Japanese: 《背後より刺され、失血死》
English: ...the cause of death was due to blood loss from a stab wound in her back.

Japanese: 傷の状態から、被害者は背中から 刺されたことが判明している！
English: This proves that the victim was stabbed from behind, not from the front!

Japanese: あやめさん！
English: Sister Iris!

Japanese: また１つ、あなたは ムジュンを重ねたのです！
English: It appears another seed of doubt has sprouted from your testimony!

Japanese: きゃあああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: い‥‥いったい‥‥ これは、どういうことですかッ！
English: What...!? What is the meaning of this, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ カンタンなコトさ。
English: Ha...! It's simple.

Japanese: 『人間には《裏》と《表》がある』 ‥‥それだけのコト、だぜ。
English: People are like books. We've all got a front and a back. You get my drift?

Japanese: あの。それだけのコト、 なのですか？
English: Umm, is that all you have to say?

Japanese: 『背に腹はかえられない』 ‥‥そう言ってもいい。
English: I can also say that darkness loves to play with the human mind.

Japanese: 証人の証言を、コトワザで かたづけないでください！
English: Could you please knock it off with the cheesy proverbs and illogical metaphors already!?

Japanese: とにかく！　証人の証言には 不自然な点が多すぎます！
English: The point is, too much of this testimony just doesn't make sense!

Japanese: 武器であるツエを捨てたこと。 争ったふたりが親子だったこと。
English: Throwing away a useful staff, the people fighting being mother and daughter...

Japanese: さらに、被害者が腹部を刺された という証言も、マチガイでした！
English: And now, she falsely claims the victim was stabbed in the stomach!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥たしかに、 あやふやな点が多いですな‥‥
English: Hmm... There certainly are some inconsistencies.

Japanese: あやめさん。 ‥‥いかがですかッ！
English: Well, Iris... How about it!?

Japanese: そ、それは、きっと‥‥
English: W-Well, it's just...

Japanese: きっと、アンタがアマすぎるん だろうぜ。まるほどう‥‥
English: If you ask me, you're just being too naïve about the whole thing.

Japanese: なんですって‥‥？
English: What do you mean...?

Japanese: コーヒーの苦味には、２５３種類 あると言われている。しかし‥‥
English: There are 253 distinct types of bitterness in coffee...

Japanese: それを味わいわけるには、全神経を 集中させなけりゃならねえ。
English: But to pick out each one requires total concentration and the use of all the senses.

Japanese: アンタ‥‥集中してたかい？ この証人の、証言に。
English: Were you really concentrating on what this witness actually said?

Japanese: ゴドー検事！ どういうコトですかッ！
English: Prosecutor Godot! Explain yourself!

Japanese: ‥‥証人は、ことあるごとに こう証言している。
English: The witness was quite unambigious about her own ambiguities when she said...

Japanese: 《中庭は暗くて、 よく見えなかった》‥‥と。
English: ...that the garden was dark and she couldn't see clearly.

Japanese: 人間が、ものを見るのに必要な ものは、たった１つ。
English: A human needs one thing to see clearly.

Japanese: それは、《光》‥‥だぜ。
English: And that is... light.

Japanese: ‥‥ひかり‥‥
English: Light...

Japanese: ところで、知ってるかい？
English: By the way, did you know?

Japanese: ‥‥奥の院で、修験者の 修行がある晩は‥‥
English: The Inner Temple has a rule that on nights when an acolyte is there training...

Japanese: かならず、中庭の灯ろうに 火を入れるキマリがあるそうだ。
English: ...the stone lantern in the garden is to always be lit.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥中庭には、大きな 灯ろうがあったな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I did wonder what that stone lantern was there for...)

Japanese: それが本当ならば‥‥
English: Well if that's true...

Japanese: 灯ろうの明かりで、現場は ハッキリ見えたはずでは‥‥？
English: ...shouldn't the witness have been able to see the crime more clearly?

Japanese: ‥‥そうなるハズだったのさ。 しかし。住職の証言によると‥‥
English: ...Normally yes, Your Honor. But according to the head nun, Sister Bikini...

Japanese: 事件当夜、灯ろうには 火をともすことはできなかった。
English: ...on the night of the crime, it was impossible to light that stone lantern.

Japanese: “できなかった”‥‥？
English: Impossible...?

Japanese: 長い間、使ってなかったため、 シンがダメになっていたそうだ。
English: It hadn't been used in a long time and the wick was no good.

Japanese: つまり‥‥あの晩、中庭は、 ほとんど闇に近かった。
English: In other words... It had to be nearly pitch black in the garden that night.

Japanese: 修験堂からもれる、わずかな 光があっただけ、なのさ。
English: There could have been a faint light coming from the Training Hall, but that's all.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったのですか‥‥
English: Most enlightening...

Japanese: これで、すべてのムジュンは 解決するだろうぜ！
English: Yes, that illuminating fact has chased all the contradictions away!

Japanese: ‥‥ツエを闇に落とせば、 見えるはずがなかった。
English: ...If the staff was dropped, it would be difficult to see.

Japanese: 娘のカオが見えなかったのも、 闇の中だったせい、だ。
English: It also explains why they didn't recognize each other.

Japanese: 闇にひそむ魔物は、見えない。 ‥‥それが、人間の限界なのさ。
English: We can't see the demons that lurk in the night... That's why humans are weak.

Japanese: そうだろう、まるほどうッ！
English: Isn't that right, Trite?

Japanese: うおおおおおおおッ！
English: Nooooooooooooooo!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にィッ！
English: Order! Order! Ordeeer!

Japanese: 裁判長。‥‥その灯ろうの データをくれてやるぜ。
English: Here, Your Honor. Let me present the stone lantern into evidence.

Japanese: アンタのアタマに、真実の 灯をともしてくれるだろうさ。
English: Maybe it will rekindle the flame of truth in your mind.

Japanese: 証拠品《灯ろう》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Stone Lantern added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （な、なんだ？ ダマりこんで‥‥）
English: (Why is the judge just sitting there with that look on his face...?)

Japanese: ‥‥どうした？　ジイさん。 明かりが強すぎたかい‥‥？
English: ...What's wrong, Your Honor? Was that flame too hot?

Japanese: ‥‥こ、この灯ろう‥‥ 何か、書いてありますッ！
English: Th-This lantern... There's something written on it!

Japanese: これは‥‥ ち、血文字、ですかッ！
English: Wh-Why... It's written in blood!

Japanese: （しまった‥‥！）
English: (Oooh boy...!)

Japanese: （‥‥裁判長は、まだ 知らなかったんだっけ‥‥）
English: (So the judge didn't know about that yet...)

Japanese: ‥‥血文字‥‥だと？
English: Written... in blood?

Japanese: こ、これは‥‥これはッ！
English: It... It says...!

Japanese: 逆さまに《マヨイ》と 書いてありますッ！
English: It says, "Maya" upside-down!

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥！
English: Wh-What the...!?

Japanese: そう‥‥、だったのですね‥‥
English: Oh yes... That's right...

Japanese: ‥‥真宵さまに追いつめられた エリスさまは‥‥
English: After being cornered and then stabbed by Mystic Maya...

Japanese: 刺されたあと、すぐには お倒れになりませんでした。
English: Mystic Elise didn't fall down right away.

Japanese: きっと‥‥後ろに回した手で、 その文字をお書きになった‥‥
English: She must have been writing that on the stone lantern behind her...

Japanese: みずからの身体から あふれ出す、‥‥血で！
English: ...with the blood that was draining out of her body!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥。 どうやら、そのようですな‥‥
English: Hmm... It certainly looks that way...

Japanese: ちょ‥‥ちょっと待ってくれ！
English: Hang on... Hang on just a minute!

Japanese: アンタたち‥‥いったい、 なんの話をしてるんだ？
English: What are you all talking about!?

Japanese: な‥‥“なんの話”と 言われましても‥‥
English: What do you mean, "What are we all talking about?"

Japanese: 血文字ですよ！　灯ろうに 書かれている‥‥
English: We're talking about the message written in blood...

Japanese: ば‥‥バカなッ！
English: Hmph... Nonsense!

Japanese: この灯ろうに‥‥ 何か書いてある、ってのか！
English: This lantern... It's as clean as a whistle!
(C-Could it be...?)

Japanese: （“見えない”のか‥‥？ この血文字が‥‥！）
English: (He can't see the bloody writing at all...!?)

Japanese: （‥‥そういえば、きのう‥‥）
English: (Now that I think of it, he did say something to me yesterday...)

Japanese: ‥‥オレの目は、とっくに イカれちまっているのさ。
English: My eyesight is pretty messed up.

Japanese: こんなデカいメガネをブラ下げて いても‥‥完全には見えねえ。
English: Even with these huge goggles on my head, I still can't see everything.

Japanese: （‥‥“完全には見えない” こういうことだったのか‥‥）
English: (So that's what he meant by that...)

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、これは 重要な手がかりです。
English: ...In any case, this is obviously an important clue.

Japanese: 事件当時、現場は暗かった。 そして‥‥
English: We now know that the crime scene was dark...

Japanese: 被害者は、このような文字を 書き残していた‥‥！
English: ...and that the victim scrawled this message on the stone lantern!

Japanese: いかがですか、ゴドー検事！
English: Well, Mr. Godot? Anything further?

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事？
English: M-Mr. Godot?
Errr, umm...

Japanese: それじゃあ‥‥ 次に進むとするぜ。
English: OK then... Let's move on.

Japanese: （あのゴドー検事が、 動揺している‥‥）
English: (Godot is literally shaking, and somehow, I don't think it's from caffeine overdose.)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、とりあえず 犯行は立証されたようです。
English: ...I believe it has now been established...

Japanese: 《綾里　真宵》による、 天流斎　エリス殺害が‥‥
English: ...that Ms. Deauxnim was killed by Maya Fey.

Japanese: （そんな‥‥バカな！）
English: (Th-That's... just wrong!)

Japanese: ‥‥そこで、次に登場するのが‥‥ アンタ、ってワケだ。
English: ...Now it's time to turn our attention to you.
...Yes, sir.

Japanese: 被害者が倒れたあと、アンタは “あること”をしたらしいな。
English: After the victim died, you did something, didn't you?

Japanese: その話をしてみな。 ‥‥聞いてやるぜ。
English: Let's hear it. ...We're all ears.

Japanese: ～あやめの《後始末》～
English: -- Sister Iris's Cover-Up --

Japanese: エリスさまが亡くなって‥‥ 私、真宵さまに声をかけました。
English: After Mystic Elise died... I called out to Mystic Maya.

Japanese: 倉院流の家元をつぐお方を、お守り しなければ‥‥そう思ったのです。
English: I thought it was my duty to protect the future Master of the Kurain Tradition.

Japanese: だから‥‥私がひとりで、死体を 奥の院から運びだすことに‥‥。
English: So I removed the body from the Inner Temple by myself...

Japanese: おぼろ橋の上を、死体を 引きずって、わたりました。
English: I dragged it behind me all the way across Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: そして、スノーモービルで 死体を葉桜院に運びこんで‥‥
English: Then I used the snowmobile to carry it back to Hazakura Temple and...

Japanese: 死体の傷をかくすため‥‥ 七支刀で、細工をしたんです。
English: ...I used the Shichishito to alter the way the wound looked.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたが、死体を運んだ‥‥
English: ...So you moved the body.

Japanese: ええ‥‥。私、葉桜院で 育てられましたので‥‥
English: Yes... I was raised at Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: 綾里家のみなさまに、 恩がえしをしたかったのです。
English: I owe a great deal of thanks to the Fey clan.

Japanese: ‥‥それなのに、まさか‥‥
English: But even so... I never imagined...

Japanese: あのエリスさまが 綾里　舞子さまだった、なんて‥‥
English: ...that Elise Deauxnim was actually Misty Fey!

Japanese: 私‥‥なんということを‥‥！
English: I've... I've committed a terrible sin!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ 《運命のイタズラ》ですなあ。
English: Hmm... A terrible trick of fate.

Japanese: （《イタズラ》で済む ハナシじゃないけど‥‥）
English: (I believe you're looking for "twist of fate", Your Honor.)

Japanese: 私、死体を始末してから 奥の院に戻るつもりでした。
English: I intended to return to the Inner Temple after taking care of the body...

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: あの住職に目撃された、 ってワケかい‥‥
English: You were spotted by the head nun, correct?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥それで、戻れなく なってしまったのです。
English: Yes... And that's why I couldn't go back.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、スジは 通っているようですな。
English: ...Your story makes sense, I suppose.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 ‥‥尋問を。
English: Mr. Wright, go ahead with your cross-examination.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: ...Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～あやめの《後始末》～
English: -- Sister Iris's Cover-Up --

Japanese: エリスさまが亡くなって‥‥ 私、真宵さまに声をかけました。
English: After Mystic Elise died... I called out to Mystic Maya.

Japanese: 倉院流の家元をつぐお方を、お守り しなければ‥‥そう思ったのです。
English: I thought it was my duty to protect the future Master of the Kurain Tradition.

Japanese: だから‥‥私がひとりで、死体を 奥の院から運びだすことに‥‥。
English: So I removed the body from the Inner Temple by myself...

Japanese: おぼろ橋の上を、死体を 引きずって、わたりました。
English: I dragged it behind me all the way across Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: そして、スノーモービルで 死体を葉桜院に運びこんで‥‥
English: Then I used the snowmobile to carry it back to Hazakura Temple and...

Japanese: 死体の傷をかくすため‥‥ 七支刀で、細工をしたんです。
English: ...I used the Shichishito to alter the way the wound looked.

Japanese: （信じたくないけど‥‥ どうやら、まちがいない）
English: (I don't want to believe it, but I don't think my logic is failing me...)

Japanese: （彼女は‥‥真宵ちゃんに、 罪を着せようとしている！）
English: (...Iris is trying to pin the murder on Maya!)

Japanese: （なぜ、彼女がそんなことを するのか‥‥？）
English: (But why would she want to do that...?)

Japanese: （答えは‥‥１つしか 考えられない、か‥‥）
English: (There's only one reason I can think of...)

Japanese: ‥‥事件が起こったとき、雪はもう すっかりやんでいました。
English: By the time the murder took place, the snow had already stopped.

Japanese: ‥‥すべてが終わるまで 待っていたわけですか？
English: So you waited until everything was over before making a move?

Japanese: そ、そういうワケでは‥‥
English: N-No, it's not like that...

Japanese: ‥‥目の前で、イノチの やりとりがあったんだ‥‥
English: She saw someone murdered right in front of her eyes...

Japanese: 声を出せなくても、 ムリはねえだろうぜ‥‥
English: It's not surprising that she was a little timid...

Japanese: ‥‥それは、 そうかもしれませんな。
English: ...Yes, I suppose that's true.

Japanese: 私も、みなさんのイキオイに のまれてしまって‥‥
English: Sometimes when I'm watching lawyers argue back and forth with each other...

Japanese: ムダなやりとりでも、ダマって 見ていることがありますからな。
English: ...I feel so helpless that I just sit quietly and wait for them to reach a conclusion.

Japanese: （‥‥止めてくれよ！）
English: (...Isn't it your job as the judge to mediate the argument!?)

Japanese: ‥‥私に気づいた真宵さまは、 そのとき、初めて‥‥
English: It wasn't until Mystic Maya saw me...

Japanese: ご自分のなさったことに 気がついたようでした。
English: ...that it finally dawned on her what she had done.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥あなたは 知っていたのですか‥‥？
English: So then... You knew...?

Japanese: “綾里　真宵”という名前が 持っている、イミを‥‥
English: You knew the significance of the name "Maya Fey"...?

Japanese: ‥‥え、ええ‥‥。 もちろん、ですわ。
English: Y-Yes... Of course I did.

Japanese: （ちょっと、おかしいな‥‥）
English: (That's odd...)

Japanese: （ビキニさんは、真宵ちゃんの ことを知らなかったのに‥‥）
English: (I mean, Sister Bikini didn't recognize Maya's name, let alone her position...)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥あの方は 私どもにとって、特別な方でした。
English: Anyway... She was a very special person indeed.

Japanese: なぜ、そんなコトを‥‥？
English: Why did you do that?

Japanese: 真宵さまに、疑いがかかっては いけない‥‥そう思いましたの。
English: Because I didn't want Mystic Maya to be suspected in any way.

Japanese: それならば、死体を現場から 遠ざければよい、と‥‥。
English: I thought the best thing would be to remove the body from the crime scene...

Japanese: ‥‥あなたが、たったひとりで 死体を運んだ‥‥
English: You moved her body all by yourself...?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。そうです。
English: ...Yes. I did.

Japanese: 雪がなかったら、ムリだった かもしれませんけど‥‥
English: It would have been impossible if it wasn't for the snow...
(The snow, huh...?)

Japanese: よく、わたれましたね。 ‥‥あの、おんぼろ橋を‥‥
English: It's pretty amazing you could make it all the way across... That's a rickety old bridge.

Japanese: 見た目はダメですけど、 意外と丈夫なのですよ。あの橋は。
English: It looks worse than it is. It's surprisingly sturdy despite its age.

Japanese: 『バカはカゼをひかない』 ‥‥それだけのコト、だぜ。
English: It's like they say, "Idiots are too stupid to catch even a simple cold."

Japanese: ‥‥それと、もう１つ‥‥ 気になることがあります。
English: ...But there's one other thing that bothers me.

Japanese: なぜ、死体を吾童川に 捨てなかったのですか？
English: Why not just throw the body into the Eagle River?

Japanese: 葉桜院に運ぶより、ラクに 死体を始末できたはずです。
English: It would have been much easier than dragging it all the way to Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: それだけでは‥‥真宵さまに お疑いがかかると思ったのです。
English: I thought that it would still cast too much suspicion on Mystic Maya...

Japanese: 死体は、なるべく遠いところに 始末したほうがよい、と‥‥
English: That's why I tried to take the body as far away as possible...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　ニクいほど スジが通っているぜ。
English: Ha...! Makes perfect sense to me!

Japanese: ‥‥今のところは、な。
English: ...For the time being.

Japanese: それで？　橋をわたった アンタは、どうしたんだ？
English: Anyway... What did you do after you crossed the bridge?

Japanese: す‥‥スノーモービル？ （ついに、出てきたか‥‥）
English: The snowmobile? (I knew that would show up sooner or later...)

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。キーは 私が持っていましたから。
English: ...Yes, I had the key.

Japanese: 葉桜院から吊り橋まで、 それを使って来ていたのです。
English: I used the snowmobile to travel from Hazakura Temple to Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: （‥‥このへんは、前回の 審理でも問題になったらしい）
English: (This is the part that was in question the other day.)

Japanese: （‥‥もっと、詳しく 聞いてみるか‥‥？）
English: (Should I ask for more details...?)

Japanese: （‥‥いや。今は他に、 気になることがある）
English: (...Forget it, there's plenty of other things to go after in this testimony.)

Japanese: （スノーモービルのキーは あやめさんが持っていたんだ）
English: (Iris had the key to the snowmobile.)

Japanese: （不自然な点は、ない‥‥）
English: (There's nothing strange about that...)

Japanese: ‥‥よろしい。 証言をつづけてもらいましょう。
English: Very well then, let's continue with the testimony.

Japanese: あなたは、スノーモービルで 死体を運んだわけですね？
English: So you transported the body by snowmobile...

Japanese: ‥‥葉桜院の、境内に。
English: ...all the way to the Hazakura Temple Courtyard?

Japanese: そのとおりです。そこなら、 真宵さまは疑われない‥‥
English: Yes. I thought that way, Mystic Maya would never be suspected.

Japanese: ‥‥そう思いました。 そして、私‥‥
English: That's what I thought, anyway. And then I...

Japanese: スノーモービルのこと‥‥ 詳しく聞かせてください。
English: Let me hear more about this snowmobile ride.

Japanese: ‥‥あの晩は、とても 冷えこんでいたので‥‥
English: Well, it was very cold that night...

Japanese: あれを使って、橋のたもとまで 行ったのです。
English: So I rode it all the way to the foot of the bridge.

Japanese: ザンネンだが、証人‥‥
English: I'm sorry, witness...

Japanese: そうなると、１つ‥‥ おかしなことがあるぜ。
English: But now, I've got a problem with your testimony.
Excuse me?

Japanese: （‥‥ゴドー検事‥‥ どういうことだ‥‥？）
English: (Godot... What are you up to now?)

Japanese: あの晩‥‥住職は、 アンタに修行をまかせたあと‥‥
English: That night... After the head nun left the remainder of the training to you...

Japanese: 歩いて、奥の院から 葉桜院に帰っている。
English: ...she said she walked from the Inner Temple back to Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥記録には、 そう書いてありますな。
English: Yes... I'm fairly certain that's what the record indicates.

Japanese: ‥‥そのときのことを、 彼女はこう言っている。
English: Let's recall her comments on the subject...

Japanese: それにねぇ。オバサンが 歩いて帰ってみると‥‥
English: Maybe... But, when I made it back to Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: 門のトコロに、ちゃんとあったの。 ‥‥あのスノーモービル。
English: ...it was there, by the Main Gate... The snowmobile, I mean.

Japanese: ‥‥スノーモービルは、 山門の前にあった‥‥
English: ...The snowmobile was in front of the Main Gate.

Japanese: コイツは‥‥“ムジュン”って ヤツじゃねえのかなあ‥‥？
English: This is one of those contradictions, wouldn't you say?

Japanese: せ、静粛に！ ‥‥ゴドー検事！
English: Or-Order! ...Mr. Godot!

Japanese: な、なぜ‥‥あなたが、 証言のムジュンをッ！
English: Wh-Why are you pointing out contradictions in the witness's testimony!?

Japanese: ‥‥ハッキリしねえことは スキじゃねえ。‥‥それだけさ。
English: ...Let's just say I like things to be neat and tidy, OK?

Japanese: あ‥‥あの‥‥ たぶん、それは‥‥
English: Umm... Well... Maybe...

Japanese: び‥‥毘忌尼さまが‥‥ 見落としたんだと思います。
English: S-Sister Bikini missed it. Yes, maybe she didn't see it.

Japanese: み、“見落とした”‥‥？ （そんな、ツゴウのいい話が‥‥）
English: You think she missed it...? (That doesn't sound right...)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　まあ、いいさ。
English: Ha...! Whatever.

Japanese: オレも、これが決定的な ムジュンとは思わねえからな。
English: Even I don't think this is decisive enough to warrant any more time or effort.

Japanese: ただ‥‥
English: It's just...

Japanese: 証言には、注意したほうがいい。 ‥‥それが言いたかったのさ。
English: You'd better be careful in your testimony. That's all I wanted to say.

Japanese: なァ、わかるだろ？ ‥‥カワイコちゃんよォ‥‥
English: A smart little cookie like you shouldn't have any trouble understanding that.
Y-Yes, sir.

Japanese: （どういうことだ‥‥）
English: (What was that all about...?)

Japanese: （ゴドー検事が‥‥ヒントを くれる、なんて！）
English: (Why would Godot want to give me a hint...!?)

Japanese: ‥‥死体をスノーモービルで 運んだのなら‥‥
English: If you really did move her body by snowmobile...

Japanese: 雪の上には、その《跡》が 残るはずですね？
English: ...then there should be tracks left in the snow, right?

Japanese: そ、それはそうでしょうな。
English: Well yes, naturally you would expect tracks.

Japanese: 前回の法廷で提出された、 この写真‥‥
English: This picture was presented at yesterday's trial...

Japanese: ‥‥これが、そのときの 《跡》なのでしょうか？
English: ...Are these the tracks from that ride?

Japanese: え‥‥ええ。そう、だと思います。
English: Y-Yes... I think they are.

Japanese: でも‥‥‥シュプールは、 １本しか残っていませんね。
English: But... I can only see one set of tracks here.

Japanese: ‥‥それは、おかしく ないと思います。
English: ...I don't see what's so strange about that.

Japanese: 葉桜院を出たときは‥‥ 雪が降っていたはず、ですから。
English: Snow was still falling when I left Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。 雪が‥‥降っていた。
English: I see... Snow was still falling, huh?

Japanese: そして、事件が起こったとき‥‥ 雪はもう、やんでいたんです。
English: And then when the murder took place, it had already stopped.

Japanese: だから‥‥こんなにクッキリ、 跡が残っているのですわ。
English: That's why there are such fresh-looking tracks.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: いかがですか、弁護人。 今の証言は‥‥
English: How about it, Mr. Wright? What do you think about this testimony...?

Japanese: （証言を聞くかぎり、 おかしい部分はなさそうだ）
English: (Nothing I've heard sounds strange at all.)

Japanese: （ムダなところに こだわらないほうがいい‥‥）
English: (I'd better not get too worked up about it...)

Japanese: ‥‥けっこうです。 証言に、モンダイはありません。
English: ...I'm fine, Your Honor. I have no problems with her testimony.

Japanese: （‥‥事件が起こったとき、 《雪がやんでいた》‥‥）
English: (When the murder took place, the snow had already stopped...?)

Japanese: （これは、もしかすると‥‥）
English: (That doesn't make sense if you stack it up against the other evidence.)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！
English: ...Your Honor!

Japanese: ただ今の発言を、 証言に加えてください！
English: I'd like the statement Iris just made added to the testimony!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥ 事件との関連は、あるのですか？
English: B-But... Does it have something to do with the case?

Japanese: ‥‥それを立証するためにも、 証言に加えることを要請します。
English: All will be made clear if you allow her statement to be added to the record.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　おもしれえ。
English: Ha...! This should be fun!

Japanese: アンタ。《雪》のこと‥‥ ハッキリさせようぜ！
English: You. Let's get this snow business cleared up, shall we?

Japanese: は‥‥はい‥‥
English: Y-Yes, sir...

Japanese: その現場を‥‥ビキニさんに 見られたわけですか？
English: Is that what you were doing when Sister Bikini saw you?

Japanese: は‥‥はい。おそらく‥‥ そう、だと思います。
English: Y-Yes, th-that's right... She probably thought the worst...
("Probably"...? More like "definitely".)

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。
English: Hmm... I see...

Japanese: だからビキニさんは、あなたが 犯人だと思ったワケですね‥‥
English: So Sister Bikini mistakenly thought you were the one that murdered Ms. Deauxnim...

Japanese: ‥‥申しわけありませんでした。
English: ...I'm so sorry. Please forgive me.

Japanese: 私のせいで、事件をコンラン させてしまって。
English: It's my fault that this case has gotten so confusing.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmmm...

Japanese: ‥‥これまでの証言には、 多くのムジュンがありました。
English: There have been a lot of contradictions in your testimony so far.

Japanese: 今度こそ‥‥ まちがいありませんね？
English: This time... are you sure it's all true?

Japanese: ‥‥え、ええ。
English: ...Y-Yes. I am.

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき、 雪はやんでいた。
English: When the murder happened, the snow had already stopped.

Japanese: だから、ハッキリと スノーモービルの跡が残った‥‥
English: According to you, that's why the snowmobile tracks were so clear...

Japanese: そ、そうです！ まちがいありません。
English: Th-That's right! I'm certain of it!

Japanese: （‥‥ついに‥‥ 捕まえたみたいだな）
English: (I think I've trapped her this time.)

Japanese: なるほど。《雪》か。
English: I see how you think... Snow, huh...

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Wh-What is going on here?

Japanese: （もし、事件が起こったとき、 雪がやんでいたと言うなら‥‥）
English: (If the snow really had stopped by the time of the murder...)

Japanese: （あの証言には、致命的な ムジュンが生まれるんだ！）
English: (...it'd mean there's a bigger hole in her story than that movie The Grid: Revelations!)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　見せてもらおうか、 まるほどう。
English: Ha...! Well then, Mr. Trite, perhaps you'd like to share your theory with us?

Japanese: アンタの《ウデ》‥‥いや。 《人差し指》‥‥かな？
English: Let's see what's up your sleeve... or rather, at the end of your index finger!

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、葉桜院　あやめに たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Iris of Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately...

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: ...to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
